definition: The body as a lived and observable dimension of experience, especially in contemplative practice.
notes: The project prefers body as the plain default in contemporary English. Bodily frame can work in explanatory prose when the embodied aspect needs emphasis, but the entry should not be overloaded with later technical meanings not active in the immediate context. The OSF glossary confirms body as the house default and records bodily frame as a tolerated alternate.

context_rules.context: In most practical and doctrinal contexts
In explanatory prose where the embodied frame of practice needs emphasis
context_rules.notes: Allow this as a controlled alternate only when the context clearly supports it.

example_phrases.translation: one remains observing the body in relation to the body

example_phrases.notes: Shows the term in one of the project's existing untranslated compounds.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping body as the house default for the body faculty in contemplative and sense-field contexts.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'body' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'corpse' or other unrecorded alternates.
